en: Oh give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; for his steadfast love endures forever!
zh: 你們要稱謝耶和華，因他本是良善的； 他的慈愛永遠長存。

en: Let Israel say, "His steadfast love endures forever."
zh: 願以色列說： “他的慈愛永遠長存。”

en: Let the house of Aaron say, "His steadfast love endures forever."
zh: 願亞倫家說： “他的慈愛永遠長存。”

en: Let those who fear the LORD say, "His steadfast love endures forever."
zh: 願敬畏耶和華的說： “他的慈愛永遠長存。”

en: Out of my distress I called on the LORD; the LORD answered me and set me free.
zh: 我在急難中求告耶和華， 他就應允我，使我站在寬闊之地。

en: The LORD is on my side; I will not fear. What can man do to me?
zh: 耶和華是我的幫助，我決不害怕， 人能把我怎麼樣呢？

en: The LORD is on my side as my helper; I shall look in triumph on those who hate me.
zh: 耶和華是我的幫助， 我必看見恨我的人遭報。

en: It is better to take refuge in the LORD than to trust in man.
zh: 投靠耶和華， 勝過倚靠人。

en: It is better to take refuge in the LORD than to trust in princes.
zh: 投靠耶和華， 勝過倚靠王子。

en: All nations surrounded me; in the name of the LORD I cut them off!
zh: 列國圍困著我， 我靠著耶和華的名必除滅他們。

en: They surrounded me, surrounded me on every side; in the name of the LORD I cut them off!
zh: 他們圍繞著我，把我圍困， 我靠著耶和華的名必除滅他們。

en: They surrounded me like bees; they went out like a fire among thorns; in the name of the LORD I cut them off!
zh: 他們如同蜜蜂圍繞著我， 但他們要像燒荊棘的火熄滅； 我靠著耶和華的名必除滅他們。

en: I was pushed hard, so that I was falling, but the LORD helped me.
zh: 他們 極力推我，要我跌倒， 耶和華卻幫助了我。

en: The LORD is my strength and my song; he has become my salvation.
zh: 耶和華是我的力量，我的詩歌； 他也成了我的拯救。

en: Glad songs of salvation are in the tents of the righteous: "The right hand of the LORD does valiantly,
zh: 在義人的帳棚裡， 有歡呼和得勝的聲音，說： “耶和華的右手行了大能的事。

en: the right hand of the LORD exalts, the right hand of the LORD does valiantly!"
zh: 耶和華的右手高高舉起； 耶和華的右手行了大能的事。”

en: I shall not die, but I shall live, and recount the deeds of the LORD.
zh: 我必不至於死，我要活下去， 並且要述說耶和華的作為。

en: The LORD has disciplined me severely, but he has not given me over to death.
zh: 耶和華雖嚴厲地管教我， 卻沒有把我置於死地。

en: Open to me the gates of righteousness, that I may enter through them and give thanks to the LORD.
zh: 請你們為我打開正義的門， 我要進去，稱謝耶和華。

en: This is the gate of the LORD; the righteous shall enter through it.
zh: 這是耶和華的門， 義人才可以進去。

en: I thank you that you have answered me and have become my salvation.
zh: 我要稱謝你，因為你應允了我， 又成了我的拯救。

en: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
zh: 建築工人所棄的石頭， 成了房角的主要石頭；

en: This is the LORD's doing; it is marvelous in our eyes.
zh: 這是耶和華作的， 在我們眼中看為希奇。

en: This is the day that the LORD has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it.
zh: 這是耶和華所定的日子， 我們要在這一日歡喜快樂。

en: Save us, we pray, O LORD! O LORD, we pray, give us success!
zh: 耶和華啊！求你施行拯救； 耶和華啊！求你使我們亨通。

en: Blessed is he who comes in the name of the LORD! We bless you from the house of the LORD.
zh: 奉耶和華的名來的是應當稱頌的， 我們從耶和華的殿中給你們祝福。

en: The LORD is God, and he has made his light to shine upon us. Bind the festal sacrifice with cords, up to the horns of the altar!
zh: 耶和華是 神，他光照了我們。 你們要用繩索把祭牲拴住，帶到祭壇的角那裡 。

en: You are my God, and I will give thanks to you; you are my God; I will extol you.
zh: 你是我的 神，我要稱謝你； 你是我的 神，我要尊崇你。

en: Oh give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; for his steadfast love endures forever!
zh: 你們要稱謝耶和華，因他本是良善的； 他的慈愛永遠長存。
